methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $160M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Quantum Motion is a Series C quantum computing company that has raised $160M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 11 comparable companies across quantum computing—3 trading publicly (IonQ, Rigetti, D-Wave) and 8 private firms including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti, D-Wave, and Infleqtion.

Only one valuation method proved applicable. The stage-progression approach uses the $160M total raised as a proxy for current enterprise value, then applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C advancement to the subsequent funding round, yielding $272M. A peer-median approach was not viable due to insufficient same-stage or near-peer comparables—while 7 quantum computing peers exist, none met the threshold of 3 or more datapoints within the same stage, adjacent stage, and valuation tier simultaneously.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $231M (low) to $313M (high), with a median of $272M. This range reflects a ±15% sensitivity band around the stage-progression median. Key uncertainties include the absence of contemporaneous public-market multiples for true peers at Quantum Motion's stage and capital efficiency profile, and the heterogeneity of quantum computing business models, which limit confidence in any single derived multiple. Further narrowing would require either recent comparable transaction data or a cohort of Series C-stage quantum companies with disclosed valuations.
